summary: Tight second‑set The Other One with a cappella He's Gone jam
blurb: The show shines on the second set, featuring an explosive The Other One and an a‑cappella He's Gone jam. Phil Lesh delivers zone‑level bass throughout, while Touch of Grey opens the night with upbeat energy. Highlights also include Desolation Row and Loose Lucy.
review: On September 12, 1993 at Philadelphia’s Spectrum the Dead delivered a crisp, B‑side‑heavy set that builds into one of ’93’s most memorable second‑sets. After an optimistic opener with Touch of Grey, the band weaves through solid staples – Walkin' Blues, Loser and Desolation Row – before launching into a powerful jam sequence. The transition from Truckin' into an a‑cappella He's Gone is a standout moment, setting up a tight, zone‑filled performance of The Other One that rolls straight into Stella Blue, Throwing Stones and a rousing Turn On Your Lovelight. Phil Lesh’s freight‑train bass drives the groove, especially on The Other One and He’s Gone, while Jerry’s supportive lead work fuels the jams without stealing the spotlight. The evening closes gently with Knockin' On Heaven's Door, leaving fans with both high‑energy improvisation and heartfelt songcraft.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings give strong positive praise (stellar The Other One, a‑cappella He's Gone jam, Phil's zone presence) and one mixed review still notes solid performances; the lone negative comment concerns only set‑list metadata, not musical quality. Overall sentiment is clearly favorable, meriting a +1.3 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent DSBD source – crisp, clean sound that captures the full energy of the performance, especially the second‑set highlights.

key_highlights: Explosive The Other One with tight playing and a cappella He's Gone jam
Phil Lesh’s zone‑level bass driving The Other One and He's Gone
Touch of Grey opens the show with upbeat energy, setting the tone

band_performance.Jerry: Provides enthusiastic support throughout, building momentum into The Other One and contributing solid leads during jams.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers freight‑train bass work, front‑and‑center in the mix, especially powerful on The Other One and He's Gone.
